theme.source_note: Tableau has no single global theme file - aggregated from per-worksheet mark-color formats; treat as a starting palette, not an authoritative theme to clone.

limitations_encountered.issue: extract-based (.hyper) data source - row data requires a separate extraction step (tableauhyperapi -> Parquet) or repointing to the true upstream system

extract-based (.hyper) data source - row data requires a separate extraction step (tableauhyperapi -> Parquet) or repointing to the true upstream system

All 14 (15 raw; 1 dedup) Tableau dashboard actions are URL/hyperlink actions - zero filter/highlight/navigation/parameter actions. URL actions have NO semantic-model equivalent; they are a report-layer concern (WebUrl-category column rendered as a link, or Button/Image with a Web URL action). Model provides dataCategory=WebUrl on 'Fun Facts'[Links] and 'Project Badges Resume'[Links] (9 of the URLs live there). The remaining 6 URLs are hardcoded/placeholder (3 Fun Facts Resume icons whose table has no Links column: tab/twitter/linked; 2 pianobar YouTube links where data Links="link"; 1 second DTC link) and must be authored by pbi-report-builder as explicit Web URL buttons. pianobar carries TWO URL actions on one mark.

Source has 0 joins, 0 blends, and 0 filter/highlight actions, so the 6 data sources are data-independent (a spatially-composed infographic, not a star schema). Data-driven overlap analysis found exactly one genuine cross-table key: 'Fun Facts Resume'[Fact] 1:1 'Fun Facts'[Title] (5 shared values). Shipped as a single INACTIVE 1:1 relationship (documented) so it imposes no fabricated cross-filtering by default; pbi-report-builder may activate it for an optional cross-highlight enhancement. Other candidates rejected: Coursework<->Coursework Detail (near-duplicate copies, not dimension->fact); Major Minor Resume[Title] vs Coursework[Category] (keys misalign - 'Data Analysis-Statistics' != 'Data Analysis'+'Statistics', would orphan 2 of 3 majors).

'Sheet1 (Coursework)' (->Coursework) and 'Sheet1 (Coursework) (2)' (->Coursework Detail) are near-duplicate sources (same 14 Courses); Coursework Detail is the superset (adds Course Descrip, InternTitle values, Index, N). Both retained per worksheet-binding reconciliation (Favorites binds Coursework; 11/22/33/44/Coursework bind Coursework Detail) - not deduplicated into one table.

4 distinct worksheet-level calculated fields are constant string literals ('.' and ' ') used as single-mark placeholders (Tableau's way to render one decorative mark without a real dimension). Dropped from the semantic model - a decorative shape/text/image is placed directly by pbi-report-builder; no data-model equivalent needed.

Tableau auto-generates a 'Number of Records' (=1) measure per data source; 6 identically-named measures crash Power BI Desktop on load (invisible to offline TmdlSerializer). Each renamed to a unique '<Table> Row Count' = COUNTROWS('<Table>') (hidden).

All 6 sources are extract-based (.hyper) with no live upstream; materialized to CSV (data/*.csv, per parse-stage limitations) and loaded as import-mode M partitions reading a DataFolder parameter. Structure-and-data faithful; not repointed to any live system.

Q&A synonyms (culture LinguisticMetadata) were not authored: the Power BI Modeling MCP exposes object descriptions but no synonym/culture-schema write operation, and hand-authoring linguistic TMDL is error-prone. Abbreviation meanings were folded into descriptions instead (e.g. 'Abbreviated subject area', 'Internship work-stream', badge codes expanded to full names). A formal synonym set is a low-cost post-deploy enhancement.

AI data schema and AI instructions (service LSDL, no stable file-authoring contract), verified answers (not Git-supported; require report visuals), and 'Approved for Copilot' + semantic indexing (tenant/workspace runtime settings) are deferred to post-deploy service configuration - not committable in TMDL today. Descriptions + categorical domain enumeration (the highest-leverage, file-committable items) are done at 100% coverage.

limitations_encountered.item: AI readiness tooling - shared verifier absent
limitations_encountered.issue: The shared scripts/check_ai_readiness.py referenced by the agent instructions is not present in the repo; an equivalent scoped verifier was added at migrations/interactive-resume/build/check_ai_readiness.py. It confirms 100% description coverage (47/47 objects) and that all 14 categorical columns enumerate their domain. Consider promoting it to the shared scripts/ folder.
